title: Digital Clock & Countdown timer - Final Touch

content.problemStatement:
Perfect - we have completed our Project!

Here are some additional tasks that you can try out.


Can you create a display box around the `digital_clock`. We have created one such version - check out the code in the IDE
Currently - you cannot exit the  timer or the clock - it keeps going on for every. 

Can you update the code to accept a user input to exit the `digital_clock` project?
constraints: Commands consist of printable ASCII characters
The clock starts at 00:00:00 and wraps after 23:59:59
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 1000), the number of commands. Each of the next T lines contains a command string without leading or trailing spaces.
content.outputFormat:
For each command that is not exactly "exit", output three lines forming a box:
+-----------------------+
| hh:mm:ss |
+-----------------------+
where hh, mm, ss are two‑digit hour, minute and second values of the current clock time. After printing, the clock advances by one second. If a command is "exit", output a single line containing the word "exit" and terminate processing further commands.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The only command is "exit", so the program prints "exit" and stops.
The command is not "exit", therefore the current time (00:00:00) is displayed inside a box. After printing, the clock advances to 00:00:01 for any subsequent command.
First command shows 00:00:00, clock moves to 00:00:01. Second command shows 00:00:01, clock moves to 00:00:02. Third command is "exit", so the program prints "exit" and terminates.

hidden.label: maximum boundary (15 ticks)

15

solution.approach: Maintain three integer variables hour, minute and second initialized to 0. Process commands sequentially. If the command equals "exit", print "exit" and break. Otherwise print the required box using the current time, then increment the second, handling carry‑over to minute and hour with modulo 60 and 24. This runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
